prevalence.source: SBU - Sociedade Brasileira de Urologia (based on national surveys)
prevalence.trend: increasing due to rising obesity and dietary changes

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Non-contrast CT for diagnosis, ultrasound, urinalysis, SBU guidelines

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 65% successful stone passage with conservative management

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: SBU guidelines, non-contrast CT gold standard, ultrasound via SUS
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Conservative: hydration, dietary changes; Medical: alpha-blockers, pain management; Surgical: lithotripsy, ureteroscopy via SUS
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Hot tropical climate increasing risk, limited lithotripsy access outside major cities, long wait times for procedures
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: SBU guidelines, surgical treatment available via SUS, pain management accessible
